frameworks.name: action-oriented working memory
frameworks.one_line: memory representations coded by task-specific temporal and behavioral demands

frameworks.name: common-workspace working memory
frameworks.one_line: items stored in shared task-agnostic format allowing inter-item interference

core_question: Are visual items held in working memory for distinct tasks stored separately or in a common store?
key_assumptions: concurrent maintenance of two items can be probed via inter-item interference
perceptual and iconic memory stages can be dissociated from WM consolidation
manipulations: framing continuous-report task combined with embedded binary-discrimination task
similarity between framing and embedded motion directions
stage of processing: perceptual / iconic / working memory
key_findings: embedded motion direction was repulsed away from the framing item's direction
embedded item's precision was degraded by the framing item
repulsion and precision degradation scaled with inter-item similarity during concurrent WM maintenance
perceptual and iconic control conditions showed weaker repulsion and no precision interference
interpretations: inter-item interference arises uniquely within working memory, supporting a shared store
task-specific temporal and action demands do not isolate items from cross-item influence
